categoryName: Normal Flora: Role in Health and Disease

questions.topicName: Normal Flora: Role in Health and Disease
questions.stem: This anatomical illustration compares the highlighted tube connecting the middle ear to the nasopharynx in an adult versus a child under 7. What property of this structure in the child explains their increased susceptibility to middle ear infections?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is shorter, narrower, and more horizontal

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is permanently occluded by adenoids

choices.label: E
choices.text: It is longer and more vertical

explanation.summary: In young children the eustachian tube is shorter, narrower, and more horizontal, allowing nasopharyngeal flora to migrate into the middle ear and impairing drainage, which predisposes to acute otitis media.
explanation.whyCorrect: In young children the eustachian tube is shorter, narrower, and more horizontal, allowing nasopharyngeal flora to migrate into the middle ear and impairing drainage, which predisposes to acute otitis media.

questions.topicName: Normal Flora: Role in Health and Disease
questions.stem: This labeled schematic contrasts normal vaginal epithelial cells with a cell whose surface is studded with bacteria. The stippled cell shown on the right is a diagnostic hallmark of which condition?

explanation.summary: A vaginal epithelial cell coated with adherent bacteria (obscuring its borders) is a clue cell, the microscopic hallmark of bacterial vaginosis, typically due to Gardnerella vaginalis overgrowth.
explanation.whyCorrect: A vaginal epithelial cell coated with adherent bacteria (obscuring its borders) is a clue cell, the microscopic hallmark of bacterial vaginosis, typically due to Gardnerella vaginalis overgrowth.

questions.topicName: Normal Flora: Role in Health and Disease
questions.stem: This clinical photograph shows the vulvar and vaginal region of a patient presenting with intense itching, erythema, and a thick white plaque. What is the most likely causative organism?

explanation.summary: The thick, white, curd-like plaque with erythema and pruritus is characteristic of vulvovaginal candidiasis caused by the yeast Candida albicans, which overgrows when normal vaginal flora is disrupted.
explanation.whyCorrect: The thick, white, curd-like plaque with erythema and pruritus is characteristic of vulvovaginal candidiasis caused by the yeast Candida albicans, which overgrows when normal vaginal flora is disrupted.

questions.topicName: Normal Flora: Role in Health and Disease
questions.stem: This photograph of an open mouth shows a white plaque coating the tongue and oral mucosa in a patient. Overgrowth of which organism produces this appearance?

explanation.summary: Oral thrush is an overgrowth of Candida spp. in the oral cavity, presenting as a wipeable white plaque; it is often triggered by antibiotics, inhaled steroids, dentures, or immunosuppression.
explanation.whyCorrect: Oral thrush is an overgrowth of Candida spp. in the oral cavity, presenting as a wipeable white plaque; it is often triggered by antibiotics, inhaled steroids, dentures, or immunosuppression.
